entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud と SAP BTP による受注処理の自動化
entry.content:
この記事はTakeru1、masanobuと共同で執筆されています。

はじめに

企業のデジタルトランスフォーメーションを支える統合型ERPソリューションとして注目される。その柔軟性と拡張性を活用することで、業務効率化や精度向上が実現可能です。今回は、SAP S/4HANA Cloudにおける販売管理業務の自動化、効率化の実現例をご紹介します。

1.   Excelを活用した受注登録の自動化シナリオ

SAPではSAP Business Technology Platform（以降SAP BTP）と呼ばれる拡張開発基盤を提供しています。これを用いて、要件に応じた柔軟な機能を活用していただくことが可能です。

SAP BTPは会計領域や人事領域、生産領域など、どのビジネスケースにも活用可能ですが、当ブログでは営業業務に従事されている方からよく伺う要望についての活用例をご紹介します。

こちらの活用例では“営業担当者が得意先から注文をメールの添付ファイル（Excel）で受け取り、SAP S/4HANA Cloud に受注として登録するまで”のプロセスになります。

通常、メールで受け取った注文 (Excel) をSAP S/4HANA Cloud に受注として登録していく場合は、一度パソコンなどにファイルをダウンロードしたのち、マニュアルでExcelをSAP S/4HANA Cloud 内にインポートする必要があります。

SAP S/4HANA Cloud ではExcelファイルからの一括登録が標準でも可能であるため、1と業務が効率的になっていますが、例えば複数の得意先からのメールなどが来た場合その都度対応しなければならないなど、まだまだ業務の改善余地があります。

そこで、SAP BTPのRPA機能（以降SAP Build Process Automation）を活用することで更なる業務の効率化を実現します。 SAP BTPでは、 SAP Build Process Automation というサービスを利用して、RPA機能やワークフロー機能をノーコードで構築いただくことができます。

今回ご紹介するシナリオでは、次のようなステップで受注処理が行われます：

 

①受注データの受け取り（担当者: 営業担当）:

受注情報を含むExcelファイルをメールにて受け取ります。

②RPAによる受注データ登録（スケジューリングにより自動で開始）:

SAP Build Process Automationがメールボックス内にあるExcelファイルを検知し、データを読み取ります。読み取られた受注情報が自動的にSAP S/4HANA Cloudにアップロードされ、受注登録が完了します。処理が完了すると、営業担当や上長などにメールが送信されます。

③受注データの確認（営業担当者）：
SAP S/4HANA Cloudに登録された受注伝票を確認します。

以上、SAP BTP を活用した受注登録の自動化シナリオをご紹介しました。
このプロセスの大きなポイントは SAP S/4HANA Cloud 上に受注データが登録されるまで人の手が一切加わらず、人的ミスの削減に繋がる点です。また、自動化により日々入るたくさん注文を迅速に捌けるようになり、更なる業務の効率化が図られます。さらに、データの一貫性が保たれるため、後続業務の信頼性も向上します。

2.   OCRを利用した受注登録の自動化シナリオ

前項ではメールに添付されたExcelファイルをRPA機能の活用により自動で受注登録するシナリオをご紹介しました。
しかし実際の業務では得意先ごとにExcelファイルだけでなく、PDFや紙などの非構造化データとして受け取っている方も多いのではないでしょうか。
実はSAP BTPではOCR（光学文字認識）技術を活用して受注情報を自動的に読み取り、SAP S/4HANA Cloudに登録することも可能になっております。このシナリオでは、紙の注文書やPDFファイルから受注データを抽出し、手作業による入力ミスを防ぎながら迅速にデータを処理します。当然メールで受け取った場合は前項でご紹介したSAP Build Process Automationを活用することによりメールの受信からSAP S/4HANA Cloud への受注登録までを人手を介さずに行うことも可能です。
これにより、業務効率が向上し、顧客対応のスピードも向上します。

 

以上がSAP S/4HANA Cloudにおける販売管理業務の自動化、効率化の実現例のご紹介となりました。

ところで、開発であればSAP BTPに限らず、どの開発基盤でもよいのではないかと思われた方もいらっしゃるかと思います。
SAP BTP上で開発するメリットの一つには、豊富な事前定義済みコンテンツを使用することで、一から開発する工数を削減し、なおかつ運用保守効率を高めて拡張開発を実現できる点が挙げられます。例えば SAP S/4HANA Cloudと SAP BTPの接続に関して事前定義済みコンテンツを使用することが可能です。

次項では、この事前定義済みコンテンツの例をご紹介します。

 

３．事前定義済みコンテンツを利用した機能拡張

SAP BTPはここまでご紹介してきたように拡張開発基盤としてご利用いただくことも可能ですが、その他にも SAP S/4HANA Cloud等のSAPのソリューションと合わせてお使いいただくことを前提に事前定義がされたコンテンツを活用して自動化や効率化を実現することも可能です。この事前定義済みコンテンツは、特定の業務シーンにおいて SAP S/4HANA Cloud 等のSAPソリューションと合わせてお使いいただけるようにあらかじめ設定がされているもので、テンプレートのコピーによってご利用いただけます。

上図でいうと、先ほどご紹介した受注登録の自動化機能は左側の「使う」ことで実現する機能です。一方、拡張開発機能を活用して、右側のように業界や会社特有の機能を「作る」ことができます。

最後に、事前定義済みコンテンツについて、追加でいくつか例をご紹介します。

実際に提供しているコンテンツは販売領域だけでなく、購買領域や生産領域など、SAP S/4HANA Cloud の標準機能と合わせて使用できるものが複数の領域で数多く提供されています。

デモシナリオ一覧

購買・在庫管理シナリオ

  • 供給元一覧の一括更新

  • Excelからの在庫移動転記

販売管理シナリオ

  • 非構造化データからの受注の自動登録

生産管理シナリオ

  • 計画独立所要量の登録・更新

まとめ

SAP S/4HANA Cloudにおける受注処理自動化は、業務効率化と精度向上を両立する強力なツールです。これらのシナリオを導入することで、企業は受注処理における課題を解決し、デジタルトランスフォーメーションをさらに加速させることが期待されます。競争の激しい市場環境において、こうした自動化技術の活用は、持続的な成長と顧客満足度の向上に寄与します。

entry.title: Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace – Standard and Flexible Integration (5 of 7)
entry.content:
As we progress through this series exploring "Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace over individual visibility platforms," we see how SAP’s solution simplifies logistics management and strengthens supply chains through enhanced logistics visibility.  Now, let's pick up from the last installment covering the power of “insight to action” to delve into a fifth key element: "Standard and Flexible Integration."  This aspect of SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace ensures smooth information flow from adding new logistics visibility capability to a shipper’s existing IT landscape. 

And if you want to learn something about SAP’s track and trace solution – something you may not have considered before – please read on.  Here’s a preview… SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace natively integrates with industry-leading SAP Transportation Management, but SAP Transportation Management is not required to track shipments and processes with SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace.  

Reason #5: Standard and Flexible Integration

Global manufacturing and distribution companies typically rely on complex IT landscapes, including ERP, warehouse management, and transportation management systems. Integrating new capabilities into an enterprise environment can be a daunting endeavor, not only because of the potential for technological complexity but also because the business processes supported by the systems are essential to the 24/7/365 operations of the going concern.  Without the right strategy for business systems, companies seeking new capabilities to strengthen their supply chains can be led astray, chasing third party solutions that require extensive customization, ultimately sending sensitive business information outside of a secure, hardened corporate landscape.

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace is designed to seamlessly integrate with a manufacturer's core business systems, including SAP S/4HANA and SAP Transportation Management. This standard integration eliminates the need for complex custom development while reducing implementation time and cost.  Furthermore, SAP Business Network enables a manufacturer or distributor to gain logistics visibility in the context of their core business.  The word “core” is the key here.  With SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace, SAP shops maintain a clean core, while gaining the capability to track shipments and leverage reliable ETAs all in the native context of their purchase, sales, and stock transfer orders. 

But does this mean that SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace
only integrates with SAP systems?

The answer is no – SAP’s track and trace solution is not only agnostic as to the sources of shipment and process tracking data, but it is open to tracking any process, and integrating tracked-process data back into any back-end ERP, TMS, or other business system.

To summarize, SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace is designed to:

  • Model and track any process (stay tuned, we’ll discuss this more in the seventh installment)
  • Take in tracking data from any source (such as direct from carrier or freight forwarder, or from any 3rd party data provider service, etc.)
  • Integrate and synchronize data for use in any ERP, TMS, or other business system (SAP or non-SAP)

For example, a manufacturer using SAP S/4HANA can organically link their sales, purchase, or stock transfer order management processes with shipment and delivery tracking. As orders are created and managed in the ERP system, shipment tracking information is available for enhanced inventory, production and customer service management. Similarly, real-time tracking updates, including estimated times of arrival, are fed back into the ERP and transportation management systems, ensuring essential access to critical business information across the organization.

And further to the point above about non-SAP systems, as another example, SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace has been deployed across multiple non-SAP transportation management systems employed by the same shipping company, providing a unifying tracking capability for the customer across one of the most complex and active regions of the global supply chain. 

Standard and flexible integration between systems is essential for effective process management.  By providing standard and flexible integration capabilities, SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace empowers global manufacturers to leverage their existing IT investments and streamline data flow across their entire operation.  This leads to greater efficiency, improved accuracy, and enhanced, contextualized decision-making.

In the next post, we'll examine how SAP’s solution addresses the importance of a consistent and personalized user experience.  With integration into core systems and processes, it is equally essential that team members don’t leave their core environment or interact with external systems that can introduce security risk, learning curves, and inefficiency. 

Read more about how Blue Diamond Growers permeated their supply chain landscape with logistics visibility through SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace. 

Next up, reason #6: Consistent and Personalized User Experience.  Check back here for the upcoming link or subscribe to SAP Business Network for Logistics on SAP Community.

entry.title: Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace – Consistent User Experience (6 of 7)
entry.content:
Continuing along our exploration of "Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace over individual visibility platforms," we turn our attention to the often overlooked aspect of: "Maintain a Consistent and Personalized User Experience."

This aspect speaks to the fact that SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace is not only powerful tool, delivering logistics visibility in the context of the shipper’s business, but it also delivers a consistent, efficient, and flexible user experience that can be tailored to an individual’s needs.

Why is this important?  Because learning curves can be steep with other external systems – they present a risk unto themselves.  Change management is a key discipline for competitiveness, as companies adapt to new needs, new risks, and new market influences. 

Asking supply chain and logistics practitioners or senior leaders to leave their core environment to maneuver in a different system, even with single sign-on, can decrease efficiency and as well as impede time-sensitive and strategic decision making.

And why is this often overlooked?  Because we often focus on what is different when looking to improve, while taking for granted the value of consistency. 

When supply chain and logistics practitioners or senior leaders can stay in their zone, where logistics visibility such as predictive ETAs can be used in the context of their core business processes, efficiency and productivity increase and latency in risk management, issue response time, and strategic decisions falls away.  

By leveraging SAP’s core design for user interface, increasingly enhanced with AI, SAP ensures that practitioners and leaders alike gain the efficiency and insights to perform at their best.  In a competitive market, the best performing companies excel at change management. 

From a change management perspective, the aim is to add capability, not complexity.

Reason #6: Maintain a Consistent and Personalized User Experience

Global manufacturing companies leverage a mix of cross-functional teams and domain experts, often spread across different locations and departments, each requiring access to specific logistics information relevant to their roles and objectives.  Distributed, matrixed teams introduce risks and challenges for system administration as well as process execution. 

Managing user access and permissions across multiple platforms can:

  • Increase complexity
  • Burden administration with low-value, time-consuming tasks
  • Lead to inconsistencies and security risks

Executing core processes that require the proverbial "swivel chair” between systems, internal and external, can:

  • Decrease efficiency
  • Limit productivity
  • Increase risk and issue response latency
  • Risk production schedules
  • Jeopardize customer satisfaction

When gaining logistics visibility capabilities, SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace mitigates these hazards by providing a single platform with consistent user access and role-based permissions based on SAP’s core user interface design. This means administrators can define user roles and grant appropriate access levels with a familiar process, ensuring that each team member sees only the information they need. This not only streamlines the user experience but also enhances data security and integrity.

Furthermore, team members and stakeholders, regardless of their department or function, can gain access to shipment tracking and predictive ETAs in the context of their core process – no need to venture into other systems or external portals.  User preferences, saved views and filters, and read-edit rights, for example, are native to the SAP landscape.  

A consistent and personalized user experience is vital for ensuring that logistics visibility capabilities are effectively adopted and utilized across an organization.  By providing a consistent, efficient, and flexible user interface along with role-based access controls, SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace empowers global manufacturers and distributors to streamline user management, enhance security, and create more efficient and productive operations.

Check out this three minute video to see how SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace fits into SAP’s suite of supply chain logistics solutions.  


Next up, our last topic, reason #7 to choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trac over individual visibility platforms: Tailor Your Tracking Process. 

Don’t miss this last installation, where the best is saved for last.  See how you can literally track any process with SAP’s solution.  And catch this last post to gather a few more ways to explore more about SAP’s track and trace capability.  

Check back here for the upcoming link or subscribe to SAP Business Network for Logistics on SAP Community.

entry.title: Why Are Eggs So Expensive In 2025?
entry.content:
About 65% of Americans consider eggs a staple in their morning routines. However, this beloved choice may need to be reevaluated as the United States grapples with an egg shortage that has driven prices to skyrocket in January 2025. Let’s explore the reasons behind this egg crisis and what lessons we can learn from a supply chain perspective.

The egg shortage has been making headlines, primarily due to a devastating bird flu outbreak that has spread rapidly across the country leading to poultry farms losing over 20 million chickens around the country. This significant reduction together with a higher demand for eggs due to the holiday season led to a dramatic price increase. According to USDA In December 2024, egg prices soared by 36.8% compared to the same month in 2023, with projections indicating a further 20.3% increase in 2025.

As we navigate through this crisis, it becomes clear that the ripple effects of the egg shortage extend far beyond just rising prices at the grocery store. Businesses that rely on eggs—such as bakeries and restaurants—are scrambling to adapt, facing increased costs and limited availability. The question arises: how can something as seemingly small as a chicken egg reveal the fragility of your business?

And most importantly, how can businesses rethink their supply chain strategies to be better prepared for such unforeseen events?

Respond to change

Once a disruption has taken place, response management comes into play. This agility is fostered by response management systems that enable businesses to pivot quickly in the face of challenge - such as current egg shortage and respond effectively to the ongoing disruption. With a robust response management strategy, companies are better equipped to navigate uncertainty and maintain operational continuity, ensuring they can meet customer demands even in turbulent times.

Increase transparency

To sense and mitigate disruptions before they occur, businesses need solutions that enable them to anticipate unforeseen events. This is where increased visibility and transparency across the business becomes essential. By enhancing transparency across all processes, businesses can not only manage their resources efficiently but also identify possible disruptions early before they escalate, enabling swift optimization of resources and the implementation of the best solutions.

Improve Demand Forecasting

Demand spikes can occur due to different reasons be it a social media trend, change of the season or any other event. No matter what, businesses need to be prepared for peaks and troughs in demand in order to be ready to meet customers’ expectations. By proactively identifying vulnerabilities in their supply chains and implementing strategies to mitigate risks, businesses can not only overcome challenges but succeed in the face of unforeseen events.

AI itself as a tool holds significant potential for predicting events and facilitating strategic decision-making. By integrating AI into their planning processes, businesses can enhance their ability to navigate the challenges and make better informed decisions in face of any disruptive event.

This ongoing egg shortage serves as a reminder of how interconnected our business and supply chains are and how vulnerable they can be to disruptions, especially when you don’t predict them happening. However, with the right strategies and business solutions in place, businesses can not only mitigate the impact of such disruptions but also anticipate and navigate challenges and opportunities more effectively.

entry.title: Assembly Subcontracting Process in Production Planning - SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Subcontracting—a process where certain production activities or components are outsourced to external vendors while maintaining seamless integration with in-house operations. For companies leveraging SAP S/4 HANA, the subcontracting process within Production Planning (PP) offers a robust framework to manage these external operations effectively.

In this blog, we’ll explore the intricacies of the Assembly subcontracting process in SAP S/4 HANA Production Planning. We’ll cover key concepts, configuration steps, and best practices to help you implement and manage subcontracting efficiently. Whether you’re new to SAP S/4 HANA or looking to refine your existing processes, this guide will provide valuable insights to enhance your production planning capabilities.

Assembly Subcontracting: The assembly subcontracting process begins with the creation of an automatic subcontracting “Purchase Request” from MRP which then processed to purchase order, which specifies the materials and components to be sent to the vendor. Once the vendor completes the assembly, the finished goods are received back into the system, and the consumed components are automatically posted as consumed. This end-to-end process is tightly integrated with inventory management, procurement, and production planning, ensuring real-time visibility and accurate tracking of materials and costs.

Step 1: Set up Special Procurement Key

Path: SPRO > Production -> Materials Requirements Planning -> Master data -> Define special procurement type.

Step 2: Maintain Special Procurement Key in Header Material

Step 3: Create BOM for Header Material

Step 4: Requirement for Planning in MD61

Step 5: MRP Run for Header Material in MD02

Step 6: Convert the Auto Generated PRs into POs in MD04

Available stock as on 23.02.2025 is 12 PC.

Planned Requirement

02/25 – 10 PC (No PR created sufficient stock available)

03/25 – 20 PC (2 PC available, PR created for Remaining 8 PC)

04/25 – 30 PC (0 PC Available, PR created for 30 PC)

Display BOM

As mentioned in BOM, 1 Final product requires 25 PC of Raw Material

PR & PO created for 8 PC & 30 PC

8 PC Final product requires 200 PC of Raw Material

30 PC Final product requires 750 PC of Raw Material

Step 7: Issue Components to PO Material in MIGO

541 Movement Type – Transfer the stock from Unrestricted to Subcontracting Stock

Step 8: Goods Receipt for Header Material

Enter the Cost Center for components

Enter the Location for Final Product in which stock will get updated.

Components stock gets consumed once after posting the MIGO document

Final Product Stock gets updated in the mentioned storage location.

entry.title: Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP IBP Implementation
entry.content:
Embarking on a project can be both exciting and daunting. One of the key aspects of a successful implementation is ensuring the quality and stability of your solution. SAP Enterprise Support offers continuous quality checks to support this goal. 

What are Continuous Quality Checks?

Continuous quality checks (CQC) are remote services that analyze your system and/or specific situations to help reduce system risks and outline optimization potential. You will receive a report, including an executive summary with findings and guidance for improvement.

Continuous Quality Checks for SAP IBP

To support your SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain (SAP IBP) implementation project, the following continuous quality check services are available:

  1. CQC for Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP - Prepare* — The latest CQC service offering that helps review the project team’s awareness of key SAP IBP topics, ensuring that you are in the right direction at the early stage of the project.
  2. CQC Going Live Support — To help you through a smooth start of production.
  3. CQC Deployment Readiness — For a detailed analysis of your SAP IBP before your go live.
  4. CQC Business Process Performance Optimization (CQC BPPO) — For a performance analysis of your business critical process steps for optimal performance improvement.

Requesting Remote Services: When and How to Get Started

The diagram below highlights the recommended timings for requesting the CQC services, aligned with the SAP Activate Methodology phases, from Discover to Run. 

For information about CQC services for supply chain management, including how to request them, visit the Continuous Quality Checks* section of our Supply Chain Management Solutions value map*.

*To access the link and for ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to join and explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. 

Get Moving with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can get personalized guidance for your SAP IBP journey.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:

  1. Sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support (please allow 2 hours for processing).
  2. Access value maps via the Learning Hub.
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value maps for Supply Chain Management Solutions. Once in the value maps, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further assistance.

Value maps are intuitive to navigate, but we recommend scheduling an onboarding call to tailor the experience to your specific needs and highlight the most relevant content.

Got a question about value maps or our content?

  • Use the “ask questions” feature in the value maps.
  • Or, contact us at: sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

Brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support value map team for Supply Chain Management Solutions.

entry.title: Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace – Tailor Your Tracking Process (7 of 7)
entry.content:
Q: When is the end of the road not really the end of the road?

A: When you have someplace else to go.

In the final installment of this series, inspired by "Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace over individual visibility platforms," we'll delve into a seventh key aspect of SAP’s track and trace solution: "Tailor Your Tracking Process."

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace is best known for:

  • Inbound and outbound real-time shipment tracking
  • Synchronizing multiple tracking data sources, direct from carriers or freight forwarders, or through any 3rd party network
  • Impact analysis, with configurable alerts and notifications, in the context of core business process tracking for Purchase, Sales, and Stock Transfer order fulfillment

But did you know SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace:

  • Is highly configurable down to the field level for unique process requirements
  • Has native tools to customize templates or model any process from scratch
  • Can leverage the benefits of the full solution for any process, such as openness to any data source and ability to apply configurable rules for actions, alerts and notifications

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace is designed and delivered with the flexibility to adapt to practically any of the unique needs of any process in any industry.  You can leverage native tools to model and track any process to benefit from the multiple facets of the solution previously described in this series. 

Reason #7: Tailor Your Tracking Process

No two manufacturing or distribution companies are exactly alike.  Each has its own unique processes, standards, and priorities, which are essential to their brand’s competitive edge.  A one-size-fits-all approach to tracking simply won’t suffice in today’s mode of continuous innovation.  Failing to capture critical nuances and specific data points essential to a proprietary process will undermine such key elements for process tracking as measurement accuracy and proactive responsiveness.  But then again, swing too far into a tracking solution outside of your core landscape and the risks and costs of integration can sink any benefit from an external system.  

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace meets the need for configuration and customization while maintaining SAP landscape and open data-source integration. SAP’s solution provides a range of flexible configuration options, allowing manufacturers and distributors to tailor their process tracking to their specific requirements. This includes the ability to:

  • Define custom fields and statuses: You can capture specific data points relevant to your industry, product, or process, such as temperature sensitivity or batch numbers, through extensibility with other apps and data sources.
  • Create custom event types and alerts: You can configure alerts to be triggered based on specific events or statuses, such as a process reaching an event milestone or exceeding a predefined threshold.
  • Develop custom process flows: You can model your proprietary process, and define automated actions to be triggered based on specific events, such as automated process re-routing or initiation of a quality control check. 

For example, a manufacturer specializing in high-value, temperature-sensitive pharmaceuticals can configure the solution to track temperature readings throughout packaging, storage, and distribution. Or in the case of global sensor technology leader SICK AG, the solution provides real-time production tracking visibility for greater efficiency, cost savings, and process automation.  In these and many more cases, manufacturers can set up alerts to notify stakeholders if an event or status deviates from an expected tolerance.  And to go one step further, custom process tracking can leverage standard event-to-action capability to automatically initiate actions such as a quality control check or re-routing if needed for optimal responsiveness.

The ability to tailor process tracking is essential to any competitor in any industry where your process is essential to you brand.  SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace provides the flexibility and customization needed to leverage a tracking solution that aligns with your processes, standards, and priorities.  Whether leveraging out-out-the-box templates or embracing a required level of customization, you can gain a deeper understanding of your business and your supply chain, make faster, more informed decisions, and ensure the safe and timely delivery of your products to your customers, with real-time, interactive process tracking.  The take-away on this topic: Effective process management is not about simply tracking the process; it's about leveraging process insights to drive cost savings, operational excellence, customer satisfaction, and growth. 

Wait, one more point.  SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace is SAP’s strategic solution for process tracking, offering a cloud solution that not only provides native integration with SAP Transportation Management, but also is the strategic successor to SAP Event Management.  Please reach your SAP representative to learn more about how the capabilities from SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace can replace your SAP Event Management installation.  

Okay, this marks the end of our series on "Why choose SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace over individual visibility platforms."  Thank you to all who have been with this since the first post, or have picked up the journey along the way.  

As from the opening of this post, this is not the end of the road.  Here are even more ways to learn about SAP’s supply chain logistics solutions, logistics visibility in general, and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace in particular. 

  • Webinar: Transforming Logistics with Next-Generation Carrier Collaboration at Celsa
  • Inbound Logistics magazine: 10 Tips to Effective Logistics Visibility
  • Product Tour: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace
  • Coming soon: Supply Chain Logistics Capability Maturity Model for self-assessment and planning

I hope you found this series helpful.  Please feel free to connect with me on LinkedIn.  And can I field any questions you may have here right from here too.

Thank you,
     Doug

entry.title: Batch, Serial, and Quality Management in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Managing electronic components demands precision, accuracy, and stringent quality control. Without an efficient system, tracking these products can become overwhelming, leading to risks such as counterfeits, defective parts, and inventory discrepancies. SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) simplifies this process through Serial Number Management, Batch Management, and Quality Control, ensuring streamlined and efficient warehouse operations.

Serial Number Management in SAP EWM ensures precise tracking by assigning unique serial numbers to items, enabling real-time traceability, warranty tracking, and counterfeit prevention at the inventory level. Batch Management organizes stock based on production batches, utilizing FIFO and FEFO strategies to optimize inventory handling, prevent obsolescence, and improve order accuracy. Quality Management safeguards product integrity by triggering inspections upon goods receipt, blocking defective stock, and enabling returns or rework to ensure only high-quality products enter production or reach customers.

Let’s dive into the configuration to achieve this requirement!

Configuration:

1. Specify Batch Level and Activate Status Management

2. Maintain Internal Batch Number Assignment Range

3. Define LE Serial Number Profile

4. Define Serial Number Profile (Other SAP Components)

5. Define Warehouse Number Dependent Serial Number Profiles

6.Serial Number Settings for Warehouse Number

7. Quality Management

  I. Generate Inspection object types versions

 II. Maintain Inspection object type

III. Maintain Follow Up Actions

IV. Assign Logistical Follow up Actions

 V. S4- Maintain Inspection Types

VI. S4 – Maintain Settings at Plant Level

VII. S4- Define Follow-up Actions for Usage Decision

VIII. S4-Edit Code Groups and code for usage Decisions

 IX. S4- Edit Selected sets for usage decisions

8. Warehouse Process Type

1. Define Warehouse Process Type

2. Define process type determination indicator

3. Determine Warehouse Process Type

9.Putaway Strategies

1. Define STSS for Put away

2. Assign Storage Types to STSS

3. Specify Storage type search sequence for put away

10. Create Material (Serial, Batch & Quality Managed)

The screenshots provided pertain exclusively to the serial, batch, and quality management parameters that must be maintained in the material.

Extend this material to Product master data in SAP EWM and extend storage location as well.

Test Case

1. Create Purchase Order- ME21N

2. Create Inbound Delivery & distribute to EWM- VL31N

With reference to the PO, an Inbound Delivery is created, including Batch and Serial Numbers. As a prerequisite, the batch must first be created using the transaction code MSC1N, as shown in the screenshot below.

 

If the vendor sends an Advance Shipping Notice (ASN) containing batch and serial number information, the system automatically creates a batch and assigns the corresponding serial numbers to the respective inbound delivery. In this scenario, manually created a batch and entered the details before assigning the serial number to the respective delivery. This ensures proper tracking and alignment of batch and serial information within the system.

3. Maintain Inbound delivery in EWM- /N/SCWM/PRDI

The delivery is distributed to EWM along with the batch and serial numbers. In SAP EWM, batches and serial numbers can also be created independently. If a batch is created in EWM, it is automatically replicated in S/4HANA to maintain consistency. Similarly, serial numbers generated in EWM are also distributed to S/4HANA, ensuring synchronization between both systems for seamless inventory tracking and management.

As soon as the goods receipt is posted in SAP EWM, the system automatically generates an inspection document, as shown in the image below. This inspection document is created because the product is quality-managed, ensuring that it undergoes the necessary quality checks before being released into inventory.

4. Inspection Lot- QA32

Since this product is batch-managed, you can search for the inspection lot using the batch number when making a usage decision. Once usage decision saved in inspection lot, An warehouse task will be created in EWM for put away.

5. Warehouse Monitor- /N/SCWM/MON

The warehouse tasks are generated based on the follow-up action configured in Step 7 (IV). The assigned Warehouse Process Type within the follow-up action triggers the creation of these tasks. Once these tasks are confirmed, the products are efficiently moved to their designated storage bins, ensuring seamless warehouse operations and accurate inventory placement. If the products are rejected, they will be moved to a scrap storage type. However, if they are returnable, they will be transferred to a quarantine storage area for temporary holding before being returned at a later stage.

In conclusion, managing electronic components efficiently requires a system that ensures tracking, quality control, and accurate inventory management. SAP EWM helps achieve this through Serial Number Management, Batch Management, and Quality Control, making warehouse operations smoother and more reliable. By using these features, businesses can reduce risks, prevent counterfeits, minimize waste, and improve efficiency. This ensures that only high-quality products make it to customers, strengthening the overall supply chain.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2502
entry.content:
Welcome to the release update blog of SAP Asset Performance Management for 2502. We're excited to share various enhancements in Asset Risk Management, Asset Reliability Engineering, Asset Data and Information Management, and Asset Health Monitoring that we believe will greatly improve your experience. Here's a list of the most important innovations:

  • Assign failure mode to Risk & Criticality Assessments
  • Displaying additional details such as 'Maintenance Plant', 'Planning Plant' on technical objects
    in Risk & Criticality Assessments 
  • Updated ‘Publish’ role in Risk & Criticality Assessments 
  • Create Recommendations without assessments with failure data
  • Enhancements to Reliability Assessments
  • Update on authorization with respect to Reliability Assessments 
  • Enable attribute-based authorization on Strategy Assessment for Classes
  • Efficiently manage your industrial system structure with new ‘Export and Import' options
  • Industrial system navigation with ‘Expand All’ and ‘Collapse All’ features
  • Download a pre-formatted Microsoft excel template to build a system hierarchy
  • Configure initial alert status and alert creation timestamps from rules
  • Enhancing rules with flexible user notifications
  • Multi-language support for rules attributes
  • Adding 4 new languages in SAP Asset Performance Management

Asset Risk Assessment

Assign failure mode to Risk & Criticality Assessments

Risk & Criticality Assessments are extended so that you can assign one or multiple  failure modes to each impact of a technical object to convey risk and potential failures. This enables you to run a failure mode centric paradigm in end-to-end asset management.

Key features

  • Display linked catalog profile in the Assignments tab.
  • Assign one or multiple failure modes against each impact assessed: Provides an F4 help selection field in the Assessments tab to select one or more failure modes per impact.
  • Apply To option: If all technical objects have the same catalog profile, the failure mode is assigned or copied only once when assessing multiple objects or when using the 'Apply To' option.

For more information, see Assessing Asset Criticality Based on the Risk Score.

Picture: When performing a Risk & Criticality Assessment, you can assign Failure Mode on each Impact by using F4 help.


Displaying additional details such as Maintenance Plant, Planning Plant on technical objects in Risk & Criticality Assessments 

Risk & Criticality Assessments now display additional details pertaining to the technical objects in the ‘Assignment’ tab. The additional details shown are

  • Technical Object Status
  • Equipment/Functional Location Category
  • Object Type
  • Structure in which the Technical Object is part of or installed
  • Maintenance Plant
  • Planning Plant

These additional details are also supported with ‘Export/Import’ of Risk and Criticality Assessments.

Updated ‘Publish’ role in Risk & Criticality Assessments 

We’re enhancing the authorization for Risk & Criticality Assessments. It is designed to differentiate the authorization on user roles so that it clear task separation is possible. Users with the RiskCriticalityAssessment_Publish  role can:

  • Release assessments.
  • Mark assessments as Obsolete.
  • Revert Obsolete status to previous state.


Asset Reliability Engineering

Assign failure data to Recommendations without assessments

We’re extending ‘Manage Recommendations’ application by enabling creation of Recommendations without assessments with failure data such as failure modes, failure effects and causes. This enhancement is part of our commitment to enable failure mode centric paradigm in end-to-end Asset Management.

Key features

  • Enable failure data assignment for recommendations created without assessments
  • Assign one or multiple failure modes, failure effects, and causes
  • Search, filter and list the recommendations based on failure modes

Enhancements on Reliability Assessments

We’re providing continuous enhancements on Reliability Assessments to improve usability. In release 2502, you can experience the followings. 

Key features

  • Support filtering of Strategy Assessment for Classes based on operating context and condition
  • UI improvement to Strategy Assessment for Classes (Similar to RCM/FMEA Assessments)
  • Provide free texts/notes while building failure data hierarchy in reliability assessments

Picture: You can filter Strategy Assessments for Classes per Operating Context and Condition.

Update on authorization with respect to Reliability Assessments 

After the 2505 release, the permissions for releasing assessments will change. Users with the FMEAAssessments_Create and FMEAAssessments_Update roles will no longer have access. Instead, users with the FMEAAssessments_Publish role will be granted the ability to release assessments. The same applies to RCM Assessment roles such as RCMAssessments_Create and RCMAssessments_Update. Strategy Assessments roles are affected in the same manner. You can read more details in SAP help. 

  • FMEAAssessments_Publish role: See 3567655 
  • RCMAssessments_Publish role: See 3567615 
  • StrategyAssessmentforClasses_Publish role: See 3567652

Enable attribute-based authorization on Strategy Assessment for Classes

‘Manage Strategy Assessment for Classes’ introduces attribute-based authorization based on ‘Planning Plant’ and ‘Maintenance Plant’ fields. This attribute-based authorization checks to ensure that information is only accessible to authorized users.

Key features

  • Update: Only users authorized for the specific Planning Plant and Maintenance Plant will be able to update these fields.
  • View: Users without authorization for the specific Planning Plant and Maintenance Plant combination will be able to view the assessment and recommendations. This allows you to review the information before deciding whether to proceed with copying.

This provides the necessary authorization and effective controls to support central and regional Strategy Assessment for Classes as Templates and Assessments.

Picture: Without having the relevant planning plant and maintenance plant assigned, it will not be possible to update the strategy assessment for classes. 

Asset Data and Information Management

Efficiently manage your industrial system structure with new ‘Export and Import’ options

Managing complex industrial system hierarchies is now easier than ever with our new Export and Import options available in the System Structure dropdown under the Structure tab. This functionality is designed to save time and enhance efficiency by streamlining the process of building and integrating system hierarchies.

Key Features

  • Export system structure: Build and export the hierarchy of your industrial systems. This feature allows for a structured and detailed view, facilitating the sharing and documentation of system configurations.
  • Import system structure: Seamlessly import existing industrial system hierarchies using Excel files. This capability ensures quick integration of system data, maintaining consistency and reducing manual entry errors.

Previously, building an industrial system required manually creating each system and its elements one by one. Now, with the 'Import System Structure' feature, the process is streamlined

  1. Initiating import
    Navigate to the 'Import System Excel' option, click 'Browse' to select your Excel file containing the system hierarchy details, and start the import process.
  2. Asynchronous processing
    The Job Manager handles the import asynchronously. You can track the status as 'In Process', 'Completed' or identify any issues if marked as 'Failed'. This transparency ensures you are informed of the process at each stage.
  3. Automatic system creation
    Upon completion, the system automatically creates the entire hierarchy using the imported Excel data. This includes all general information, language settings, plant details, and system elements. The tree structure with top nodes, functional hierarchies, products, and locations is compiled, offering a comprehensive view of the entire system.
  4. Verification and expansion
    Users can expand the hierarchy to view detailed node information and verify the accuracy of the imported data. This feature enables you to confirm that the system reflects the intended structure and details.

These export and import capabilities streamline system management, reduce errors, and save time, making it easier to maintain accurate industrial system hierarchies. For more details, see the SAP Help Portal Export or Import a System Structure.

Enhance industrial system navigation with ‘Expand All’ and ‘Collapse All’ features

Navigating through complex industrial system hierarchies has become simpler with our new Expand All and Collapse All buttons. These tools allow users to quickly view or minimize their entire system structure, enhancing efficiency and ease of navigation.

Key Features

  • Expand all and collapse all buttons
    These buttons, located in the structure tab, provide a quick and comprehensive view of your entire system hierarchy or simplify your workspace by collapsing all nodes. This is perfect for quickly locating specific system elements, especially after page reloads or re-navigation.
  • Seamless functionality with large data sets
    The system is designed to manage both small and extensive data hierarchies effectively, ensuring that users can expand or collapse structures effortlessly, even when handling a significant amount of data.

Picture: Please see above screen shot.

Download a pre-formatted Microsoft excel template to build a system hierarchy

Our latest feature simplifies the process of organizing and managing industrial system hierarchies through a downloadable, pre-formatted Microsoft excel template. Accessible via the "Download Template" button on the list page, this tool is designed to enhance efficiency and organization.

Key Features

  • Structured design: The template offers a structured approach to building a hierarchy of your industrial systems, ensuring consistency and accuracy across your data.
  • Comprehensive sheets: The Excel file includes multiple sheets to cover all aspects of your system's hierarchy:
    • System Header Details: Capture essential details of your system.
    • Top Node: Define the principal node of your hierarchy.
    • System Element Hierarchy (or Aspect Hierarchy): Organize the elements or aspects within your system.
    • Relationships (or Product Individual): Map out relationships or individual product details.
    • Product Individual: Detail each unique product within your hierarchy.

For more detailed guidance on managing industrial systems using the template, see the SAP Help Portal Manage Industrial Systems.

Asset Health Monitoring

Configure alert status and alert creation date from rules

‘Manage Rules’ in release 2502 introduces new enhancements designed to give you greater control over your alert workflows. With the latest updates, you now have the ability to configure the initial alert status and alert creation timestamp directly from your monitoring rules, making your processes more efficient and tailored to your needs. (* APM based on embedded IoT only)

Key features

  • Alert status customization
    With the new 'Alert Status' field, you can set whether alerts generated by a rule should begin in the 'New' status or 'In Process' status. This flexibility allows you to align alerts with your team’s workflow, ensuring that alerts are handled appropriately from the moment they're created. Based on this it is for example possible to create alerts in status 'In Process' right from the beginning in case a parallel notification has been created at the same time.

  • Control alert creation date
    The 'Alert Creation Date' feature gives you the power to specify the timestamp for alerts generated by rules. This control for example ensures that alert creation timestamps accurately reflect the timing of the conditions that triggered their creation, enhancing your ability to track and respond to events.

Picture: Use control + Space to get suggestions in the fields for Alert Status and Alert Creation Date. 

For detailed instructions on how to implement these features, see the SAP Help Portal Creating a Monitoring Rule

Enhancing Rules with flexible user notifications

We’re providing users with greater flexibility in receiving and managing notifications. The introduction of the "User Notification" feature is designed to streamline communication and improve workflow efficiency by providing customizable notification options for your rules.
(*APM based on embedded IoT only)

Key Features

  • Customizable notification body: Note in email notification’s body is fully customizable including dynamic values and hyperlinks. This flexibility ensures that emails convey the necessary context and details for recipients to take appropriate action.
  • Default email body include clickable hyperlinks to created alert and affected technical object.
  • Recipient selection: Users must select notification recipients from those maintained within the Identity provider associated with APM. This ensures alerts are directed to the correct individuals and maintains compliance with user management policies.
  • Flexible delivery options: Notifications can be delivered as FLP notifications or as emails, depending on user preferences. This flexibility allows users to choose the most convenient mode of alert delivery, enhancing usability and reducing notification fatigue.
  • User-controlled subscription: Users can manage their notification preferences, deciding whether to receive alerts as FLP notifications, emails, or a combination of both. This control over alert delivery ensures that users receive notices in a way that best fits their workflow and priorities.

Multi-language support for rules attributes

Our latest update provides a practical solution for organizations operating in multilingual environments by allowing users to translate all attributes used in rule conditions. This new feature supports several additional languages, including Italian, Indonesian, and Norwegian, among others.
(*APM based on embedded IoT only)

  • Improved accessibility: Translating data set attributes ensures that users can interact with rule conditions in their native languages, reducing misunderstandings and improving data management accuracy.
  • Enhanced collaboration: This update supports better collaboration among international teams by enabling everyone to work in their preferred language, fostering clearer communication and efficient workflows.

By clicking the translation button within the data set interface, users can easily add translations for rule condition attributes. This simple process helps tailor the system to meet diverse language needs, ensuring clarity for all team members.

 

Supporting 4 new languages across SAP Asset Performance Management

As mentioned above in Rules, more languages are added in SAP Asset Performance Management in the release 2502. This applies to all other apps across Asset Risk Management, Reliability Engineering, and Asset Data and Information Management. These languages will be supported in both short and long texts (UI & APIs), including Export / Import excel.

  • Norwegian
  • Dutch
  • Italian
  • Indonesian

This is the release highlights I prepared for the release 2502. Hope this provides you good overview. You can also refer to live webcast or recordings to get the details.

You can subscribe/register the webcast sessions as below.
SAP Digital Supply Chain Engagement Hub - Intelligent Asset Management & Field Service Management

Keep In Touch

Here's a quick reminder how to keep in touch with all things APM:

  • Check out all past and present Intelligent Asset Management webcasts here. If you register for an IAM webcast, you will be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter. 
  • Check some of our recent blogs:
    • What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2501 - SAP Community
    • Why Failure-Mode Focus Matters in Intelligent Asset Management 
    • SAP Asset Performance Management Embedding Cumulocity IoT to Drive Innovations in IoT and AI
    • How AI-based Anomaly Detection empowers reliability engineers in SAP Asset Performance Management
  • Subscribe to What's New on the SAP Help Portal
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session

entry.title: Why Collaborative Maintenance?
entry.content:
In today's world, many companies are moving from single-tier to multi-tier supply chains. While this shift provides flexibility, cost advantages, and resilience, it also introduces challenges, particularly around connectivity and visibility across suppliers, manufacturers, and service contractors.

  • Data Silos & Inconsistent Standards: Different systems among suppliers complicate data exchange and hinder interoperability. This can lead to significant delays and miscommunications.
  • Lack of Real-Time Visibility: Companies struggle to track assets and maintenance across tiers, resulting in potential disruptions and inefficiencies in the supply chain.
  • Business Partner Onboarding & Integration: Connecting new suppliers is complex, especially for smaller ones with limited technology infrastructure. This complexity can slow down the integration process and affect overall productivity.
  • Collaboration & Communication Gaps: Misalignment between tiers leads to errors and inefficiencies. Effective communication and collaboration are crucial to ensure smooth operations and minimize misunderstandings.

 

How to Overcome These Challenges?

 

SAP Business Network addresses multi-tier maintenance connectivity challenges by offering a collaborative platform for asset operators, OEMs, spare parts suppliers, and service contractors. This innovative platform streamlines maintenance collaboration across various supply chain tiers, enhancing efficiency and reducing potential disruptions.

 

 A multi-tier maintenance network creates value for both asset operators and service contractors by improving visibility, collaboration, and efficiency in maintenance operations. Here’s a breakdown of the value proposition from both perspectives:
 
Asset Operators
  • Automatically share work orders with qualified service contractors to ensure quick response times by connecting with multiple contractors and collaborating on a single source of truth. This approach streamlines communication, reduces delays, and ensures that all parties have access to the same accurate information.
  • Reduce maintenance data management costs by improving the efficiency of data handover processes.
  • Track the exact progress of a maintenance job in real time. Updates include job start time, in-progress status, completion, and documents.
Service Contractors
  • Expand your market reach by connecting with multiple asset operators on a single platform, enhancing your business opportunities and growth potential.
  • Improve wrench time by providing centralized access to all maintenance information, ensuring continuous collaboration among asset ecosystem partners and enhancing overall efficiency.
  • Enhance maintenance practices with accurate and comprehensive technical data to ensure efficiency and accuracy in all maintenance operations.

 Conclusion: Why Use SAP Business Network for Maintenance?

  • Improve asset data accuracy & completeness: Remove the data silos and collaborating on a common model taxonomy and create a consistent understanding.
  • Improve wrench time: Provide centralized access to all maintenance information with continuous collaboration among asset ecosystem partners.
  • Reduce maintenance data management costs: Improve efficiency of data handover from asset owners to service providers and reduce data maintenance and validation.
  • Reduce un-planned downtime or outages: Enable effective collaboration between the operator and service provider on equipment failures and maintenance performance.
  • Improve customer satisfaction: Manage of the full customer lifecycle from maintenance planning, scheduling, execution, and customer service.

 

Thanks for reading the blog post and Stay tuned!

We encourage you to visit the SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration Customer Influence Portal to submit your innovation request...

We hope you enjoy these new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and Stay tuned!

Follow me on LinkedIn

entry.title: The Looming Tariff Tsunami: Is Your Business Ready?
entry.content:
“Tariff” is fast becoming the word of the year.

And the treat of tariffs is for sure the most talked about topic in boardrooms all over the world as they become “the hammer” in many geopolitical conversations!

Why?

Because over the past decades we have built often complex supply chains that span continents, delivering goods with incredible speed and competitive prices. However, this complexity also introduces vulnerabilities, and recently, tariffs have emerged as a major disruptor.

The Ripple Effect of Tariffs

When tariffs hit, the impact reverberates throughout the entire supply chain. To make any profit at all, importers need to pass some (if not all) of the increased costs onto customers, often leading to reduced demand. Retailers are then forced to rethink sourcing strategies, re-evaluate supplier relationships, and overhaul inventory management practices. The numbers can be staggering, with some estimates suggesting potential annual cost increases of thousands of dollars for consumers.

Adding to the challenge, businesses are already facing rising administrative costs (which have risen 14% from 2023-2024), regulatory pressures and the looming uncertainty of future trade policies. Leaders are actively seeking ways to mitigate these overhead expenses. While options like nearshoring or exploring new sourcing routes might seem appealing, they often prove to be short-term fixes with their own set of risks.

Beyond Reactive Measures: Embracing Technology-Driven Resilience

Instead of simply reacting to tariff changes, companies need a more proactive, long-term strategy. This is where technology, particularly advanced supply chain management solutions, comes into play. By harnessing the power of end-to-end data visibility, artificial intelligence (AI), and automation, businesses can transform their supply chains into resilient assets. They can not only withstand disruptions but emerge stronger and more adaptable:

  • Scenario Planning: Model "what-if" scenarios to test different supply chain strategies before implementation. By developing contingency plans to address potential disruptions through alternate sourcing regions, logistics routes, and pricing models you can minimize tariff exposure.
  • Alternative Sourcing Strategies: Because tariffs depend on country of origin, many companies will seek alternative suppliers in regions not impacted. In fact, nearly one-third of businesses are pursuing a dual supply chain approach where they have both global and local options. Business Network collaboration platforms not only help identify potential suppliers at risk but also provide alternative sources of supply and collaborative tools to ensure rapid and precise management of disruptions.
  • Inventory Optimization: Identifying key products that can be purchased in advance of tariffs to bolster inventory and ensuring that they are positioned in key locations across the supply chain will help minimize the impact of looming tariffs. Studies show that top-performing supply chain organizations are investing in AI and machine learning at twice the rate of their lower-performing peers.
  • Enhanced Supply Chain Visibility: Real-time tracking of demand fluctuations, inventory levels, and logistical operations, allowing businesses to swiftly adjust their strategies in response to new tariffs or other disruptions. This allows you to diversify suppliers, optimize inventory placement, and adjust logistics to minimize the impact of tariffs.

A Smarter Path Forward

In this volatile trade environment, resilience, agility, and adaptability are no longer optional – they're essential. By embracing the right technology, businesses can maintain competitiveness and ensure that tariffs don't dictate their success.

Discover how SAP Business Suite can help your business be more resilient to business challenges and responsive to opportunities.

Follow me on Twitter.

entry.title: Aluminum And Steel Tariffs: What Do You Mean My Beer Will Cost More?
entry.content:
I was watching the local news when I heard a report that hit me to the core.

A local microbrewer was explaining that they were being forced to raise the price of my favorite beer by $2 a case.

That piqued my interest, to say the least. Upon listening further, it became apparently clear the reason for the price hike was due to the rising cost of aluminum cans.

Again, I was glued to the TV to find out why.

The aluminum and steel tariffs are set to come into effect on March 12 (at 12:01 a.m. Eastern Standard Time to be precise). The tariffs will impose a 25% duty on all imports of aluminum and steel into the USA.

And that has a knock-on effect to my favorite libation! Apparently, the cost of the can I was now gently squeezing in my fist is 65% of the costs associated with my beverage of choice.

That’s 65% x 25% of a can of beer.

Talk about a sobering moment!

What are the downstream impacts?

But it is not only me that will be crying into my drink. The effects of these tariffs are significant and wide ranging.

On the beer can half empty side:

  • The Automotive Industry is estimating the cost of a new car could increase by between $4,000 – $12,000, which will in turn increase both the demand and price of used car prices as buyers seek cheaper alternatives.
  • Manufacturing sectors like industrial equipment manufacturers and household appliance producers are some of the hardest-hit industries, as they rely heavily on imported steel and aluminum. The tariffs increase production costs for these manufacturers, potentially leading to higher prices for consumers and decreased employment in these sectors.
  • The construction industry, which employs over 10 million Americans, is significantly impacted as increased steel cost make building projects more expensive and will potentially slow down construction activity and impact overall demand.
  • Consumer prices are expected to rise: As higher manufacturing costs are likely to be passed on to consumers, resulting in price increases for various products, including vehicles, canned products (including my beer), home appliances and construction materials.

But on the beer can half full side:

  • Recycling sector: In the short term, the tariffs may benefit the recycling industry by increasing demand for domestic sources of these metals, such as used beverage cans and containers.
  • Domestic metal industries: In the longer term, the goal is for U.S. steel and aluminum producers to benefit from reduced foreign competition, potentially leading to increased domestic production and job growth in these sectors.

These knock-on effects highlight the complex and far-reaching consequences of the aluminum and steel tariffs across various sectors of the economy and international trade relationships. Only time will tell!

Discover how SAP Business Suite can help your business be more resilient to business challenges and responsive to opportunities.

Follow me on Twitter.

entry.title: Introducing Data Extraction for PP/DS for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Background

SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling (PP/DS) capability is used to create procurement proposals for in-house production or external procurement to cover product requirements and to optimize and plan the resource schedule and the order dates/times in detail. With the help of PP/DS, a Production Planner can reduce the lead times, increase on-time delivery performance, or increase the throughput of products and reduce the stock costs.

However, how can the Production Planners ensure that their production plans account for past trends, seasonality, and recurring bottlenecks, to drive more proactive decision-making?

In today's data-driven world, having access to accurate and timely data is crucial for making informed decisions. Depending on the complexity of the business, a production planner could be dealing with hundreds of resources, thousands of products, and thousands of partners and customers. The ability to extract production planning and scheduling data - and analyze it to uncover actionable insights - is critical for adapting future production plans and maintaining a competitive edge.

 

Introduction

As a part of the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03, four Data Extraction CDS views have been released for PP/DS:

  • Order Details
  • Order Operations
  • Pegging Relationships
  • Resource Capacity

Using the above data extraction views, you can fetch PP/DS data from your SAP S/4HANA system and load it into your data warehouse for reporting, auditing, analytics, or record keeping.

 

Data Extraction Views

Each of the four Data Extraction CDS views given below have the following inputs:

  • Planning Version
  • Start Date
  • End Date
  • Work Area
  • Customizing ID (of the Work Area)

The Start Date and End Date are both inclusive, and the results are always returned in the UTC Time Zone. If your Work Area does not have a Customizing ID, add a single space in the Customizing ID field.

Order Details

The Data Extraction CDS view C_APSOrdDetsByWorkAreaDEX (Data Extraction view for Order Details by Work Area) extracts the order details including the status, duration, quantity, date, and time.

Order Operations

The Data Extraction CDS view C_APSOrdOpsByWorkAreaDEX (Data Extraction view for Order Operations by Work Area) extracts the order operation (and phases/suboperations) details including the status, duration, quantity, date, and time.

Pegging Relationships

The Data Extraction CDS view C_APSOrdToDmndByWorkAreaDEX (Data Extraction view for Pegging Relationships by Work Area) extracts the pegging relationship details between receipts and requirements including the status, quantity, date, and time.

Resource Capacity

The Data Extraction CDS view C_APSRsceCapByWorkAreaDEX (Data Extraction view for Resource Capacity by Work Area) extracts the capacity duration details for resources on every calendar day from the start date to the end date. The capacity duration excludes the downtime duration.

 

Wrapping Up

You can use the Data Extraction CDS views to extract PP/DS data from your SAP S/4HANA system. These Data Extraction views can be consumed by applications and frameworks that can consume data extraction CDS views (for example, SAP BW, etc.).

For detailed documentation of the views, see this SAP Help page.

Please feel free to share your feedback and comments.

P.S.: To ensure that all the prerequisites are maintained in your SAP S/4HANA system, refer the SAP Note 3562680.

entry.title: Intra-Company Purchasing in S/4 HANA: A Guide to Efficient STO Processes
entry.content:
Intra-company purchasing in SAP is a powerful solution that enables seamless stock transfers and transactions between different entities within the same organization. By leveraging SAP’s robust functionalities, businesses can ensure accurate inventory management, cost control, and streamlined logistics.

This blog dives into the key steps involved in setting up intra-company purchasing in SAP. From defining the sales organization for the supplying company code to configuring delivery types and assigning shipping points, we’ll explore the essential configurations required to optimize internal supply chains. Whether you’re creating a customer in the supplying company code, extending material master data, this guide will walk you through the process to ensure a smooth and efficient intra-company purchasing setup.

Step 1: Define Sales Organization for Company Code

Path: SPRO --> SAP Reference IMG --> Enterprise Structure --> Definition --> Sales and Distribution --> Define copy, delete, check sales organization.

Step 2: Define Distribution Channel

Path: SPRO --> SAP Reference IMG --> Enterprise Structure --> Definition --> Sales and Distribution --> Define copy, delete, check distribution channel

Step 3: Maintain sales office 

Path: SPRO --> SAP Reference IMG --> Enterprise Structure --> Definition --> Sales and Distribution --> Maintain sales office 

Step 4: Maintain Sales Group

Path: SPRO --> SAP Reference IMG --> Enterprise Structure --> Definition --> Sales and Distribution --> Maintain Sales group

Step 5: Assign Sales Org. to Company Code

Path: SPRO --> SAP Reference IMG --> Enterprise Structure --> Assignment --> Sales and Distribution --> Assign sales organization to Company Code

Step 6: Assign distribution channel to sales organization

Leelamohan Mannem

entry.title: The NEW New Service Connection Process for Energy and Water
entry.content:
New service connection, a complex business
This process is a perfect example of integrating customer service, enterprise asset management and field service. Utilities have not changed how buildings are connected to the grid, however, work management operations and software solutions have improved significantly. Let me show you how the redesigned new service connection process runs with SAP service management, both from a customer and work management view. 

Reference process flow
If you compare a water connection for a single home with an electric connection for an office building, you can see both, a variation in complexity and a common pattern. We considered this to define a reference process with SAP service management. It has two main parts, a commercial phase followed by a technical phase. The main solutions are service and maintenance functions in S/4HANA Cloud private edition and Field Service Management.

Process steps

Steps 1 - 4, commercial phase:
First, during new construction, either a land developer, builder or contractor requests a new service connection. The process starts in customer service, online or with an agent. A service request is created and captures customer information, the new service location, dates and the type of request. Next is a follow-up service order for a field survey. A technician needs to assess, measure and plan the new connection design. With this information, the service agent can create a service quote and send it to the requestor.  When the quote has been accepted, the first phase, mostly commercial and customer centric, is complete.

Steps 5 - 8, technical execution phase:
In the second phase, a work manager receives the service order, plans the execution operations, equipment, material, schedules a crew and dispatches the work. A field crew performs the installation, time and material is recorded in the field, orders are confirmed. This phase, work and asset management centric, concludes when the orders are confirmed, billed and settled. The builder is informed that the connection is ready for meter installation.

Document flow
As you can tell by now, this is a long workflow, typically taking weeks. From a utility perspective, it is an 'ad-hoc service request' by an external party. That's why the backbone of this process is service with advanced execution, where service starts with a customer request and the execution is complex and linked to asset management. See also my other blog about this topic. 

Reference document flow new service connection process

What has changed and what is special?
Compared with previous solutions and releases, for example SAP ECC work management, customer service (CS), service management (PM/CS) or mobile solutions, many capabilities have changed. Let me point them out and add important remarks.

Service request and service order
The initial object is the (new) service request, followed by a (new) service order. I say (new) service to highlight the (new) service objects that became available in S/4HANA service with customer management. The service request is either created directly in S/4HANA, via an online form or from Service Cloud. Keep in mind, we do no longer use the sunset PM/CS objects, service notification PM-CS-SN and service orders PM-CS-SO.

This service request contains a customer/sold-to-party, dates, and we need a service loaction. If the functional location does not exist yet, it is created now or at least a building address is captured. Nothing new, just note that the new service objects can reference locations and equipment.

From here on there are about 10 subsequent process documents that follow the service request. They are connected via document flows, as shown in the picture above. This is very useful, copy control configuration and standard integrations automate the process.

Following the service request, the service order for the field survey uses a simple transaction type SRVO and a simple service product. I call it simple because we do not need any plant maintenance involved yet. This order is for a field technician to visit the construction site and gather information to plan the connection design. 

Field service management
The service order is dispatched to a surveyor in the field. Mind the service order to field service management (FSM) document flow, we utilize FSM rather than SAP Service and Asset Manager (SAM). The mobile FSM app uses smartforms to capture the connection specifics, for example 'residential electric service, 240V 2-phase, 200A, 15m long, above ground'. 

Service quotation
The service quotation document is key for the commercial agreement and for preparing the work management phase. The ability to quote service, including complex service with advanced execution, is new in S/4HANA. A quotation of the document type MSQ is a precursor of the service order with advanced execution of the type MSO. A different service product is used for the connection order item, now with an item category for advanced execution.

The calculation of the new connection amount payable by the builder is happening here, using item pricing. The utility can use simple or complex rules to determine a connection price. A debit request can be created for an upfront payment. 


Screen capture of a service quotation

Once the builder accepts the quotation, the maintenance service order is created automatically and visible in the transaction history document flow.

Service order with advanced execution
The service order with advanced execution of the type MSO (maintenance service order) links us to plant maintenance. Not much has changed in the linked PM order, see below, but watch the 'Service' section. This is new, it contains the link from PM back to service.  


Screen capture of a maintenance order

The maintenance order operations drive the field service activities. 


Screen capture of a maintenance order, operations


Field service management, again
FSM takes over with planning, dispatching and the field service execution. This is the heavy lifting of the service with advanced execution. Field work can include digging a trench, installing cables or pipes, setting poles and transformers, and mounting conduits and meter sockets. Field technicians record time, material, expenses and update equipment records in the mobile app.


Screen capture of the FSM dispatching board

You may have noticed, FSM works for both, simple customer centric service orders like the survey and here, for complex plant maintenance centric operations.

Completion
Once the order is technically complete, the new service, for example power or water, can be turned on. Because most services are metered, this starts the next process, a device installation in meter-to-cash. The confirmed orders are internally settled and billed. The new connection becomes a field asset, located at a customer address.

Summary
The redesigned service process is compatible with the current SAP solutions and suitable for most use cases, especially residential and commercial connections. The benefit of the new service objects is the flexibility and customer orientation. When needed for advanced execution, we use the extensive capabilities in plant maintenance. 

There are some exceptions to the applicability of this reference process. For example, a connection for an entire factory to the grid at a high voltage level would rather be seen as a capital project and managed like an investment.
